about.hero.title: About the project
about.hero.subtitle: Simple Deutsch
about.hero.description:
Simple Deutsch is a project I started when I realized one simple thing:
you can spend hundreds of hours learning a language and still not feel confident.

I went through this myself: from zero to a level where I could study and work in Germany.
Along the way, it became clear that the problem is not effort, but how learning is structured.
about.mission.title: Our mission
about.mission.lead: Make German learning practical, approachable, and measurable for everyone.
about.mission.principle.1.title: Clarity
about.mission.principle.1.desc: Simple explanations and clear goals so you know what to learn next.
about.mission.principle.2.title: Practicality
about.mission.principle.2.desc: Lessons built around real usage: speaking, listening, and writing.
about.mission.principle.3.title: Consistency
about.mission.principle.3.desc: Small, frequent practice beats occasional long sessions.
about.how.title: How it works
about.how.step.1.title: Learn core concepts
about.how.step.1.desc: Short lessons introduce essential grammar and vocabulary.
about.how.step.2.title: Practice with context
about.how.step.2.desc: Interactive exercises simulate real conversations and tasks.
about.how.step.3.title: Track your progress
about.how.step.3.desc: See clear milestones and measurable improvement.
about.how.step.4.title: Apply what you learn
about.how.step.4.desc: Use real-world prompts to build confidence and fluency.
about.impact.title: Impact
about.impact.metric.1.label: Learners engaged
about.impact.metric.2.label: Completion rate for core lessons
about.impact.metric.3.label: Average learner rating
about.values.title: Our values
about.values.1.title: Learner-first
about.values.1.desc: Design choices focused on real learner outcomes.
about.values.2.title: Evidence-driven
about.values.2.desc: We iterate based on real usage and feedback.
about.values.3.title: Accessible
about.values.3.desc: Materials for different levels and speeds.
about.values.4.title: Respectful
about.values.5.title: Transparent
about.values.5.desc: Clear expectations and honest progress tracking.
about.values.6.title: Practical
about.values.6.desc: Focus on tasks you can use today.
about.faq.title: Frequently asked questions
about.faq.1.a: Learners at all levels who want practical German for real life.
about.faq.2.a: We provide completion badges and optional assessments for progress proof.
about.faq.3.q: Can I use this for school or work?
about.faq.3.a: Yes — materials are suitable for individual learning and classroom use.
about.faq.4.a: Many lessons and exercises are free to try; premium paths unlock deeper tracks.
about.faq.5.q: How long until I see progress?
about.faq.5.a: With regular practice, learners often notice improvement in weeks, not months.
about.faq.6.a: Use the contact link in the footer or email hello@simpledeutsch.com.
about.cta.finalLead: Short articles, clear theory, and practical tools — the foundation for confidence in German.
about.sections.realLife.title: What's the problem
about.sections.realLife.p1:
Many people learn a language in a similar way:

memorize words and rules
go through topics one by one
spend a lot of time
about.sections.realLife.p2: But at some point, speaking is still difficult.
about.sections.realLife.p3:
Not because people don't try hard enough,
but because learning is disconnected from real life.
about.sections.consistency.title: How Simple Deutsch started
about.sections.consistency.p1: The project grew out of an attempt to simplify this process.
about.sections.consistency.p2:
The idea was simple:
to make learning actually useful in real life,
not something that stays only "in your head".
about.sections.fits.title: The Simple Deutsch approach
about.sections.fits.p1: Simple Deutsch is about structured theory and effective learning tools.
about.sections.fits.p2: Instead of chaotic topics, it provides a structured system: key elements of the language are selected, explained, and shown in use. The main focus is understanding how the language works, not memorizing it.
about.sections.fits.p3:
It gives a foundation that helps you see the system, express your thoughts, and work with the language more confidently.
At the same time, the result depends on the person — how consistently they apply these tools in practice.
about.sections.fits.p4:
Simple Deutsch is an attempt to make language learning
clearer, calmer, and more realistic.
about.sections.fits.p5:
Without overload.
Without the feeling that "I learn, but don't move forward".
about.sections.fits.p6: Step by step — towards actually speaking.
about.testimonials.heading: Why learners trust Simple Deutsch
about.testimonials.sub: Real learners share what works — clarity, practice, and steady progress.
about.testimonials.1.name: Anna Müller
about.testimonials.1.text: I appreciate how lessons focus on concrete tasks. That clarity meant I could practice useful phrases for work and daily life without getting lost in theory.
about.testimonials.2.name: Oleg Petrov
about.testimonials.2.text: Short, predictable lessons helped me build a routine. Small wins each day kept me motivated while adjusting to a new city and language.
about.testimonials.3.name: Lena Fischer
about.testimonials.3.text: I liked that lessons emphasize practical usage. Practicing short dialogues made speaking more natural and less intimidating.
about.testimonials.4.name: Mateo Rossi
about.testimonials.4.text: The focus on small steps prevents overwhelm. I can pick a short lesson between projects and still feel I'm progressing.
about.testimonials.5.name: Sofia Ivanova
about.testimonials.5.text: Consistency mattered most for me. Short daily practice felt achievable and slowly built my confidence to speak in meetings.
about.testimonials.6.name: Jonas Becker
about.testimonials.6.text: Practical exercises and real-world examples helped me use phrases in context. That practical focus made learning feel useful from day one.

partnerships.meta.title: Partnerships
partnerships.meta.description: Simple Deutsch is open to collaboration with everyone: individuals, teams, companies, and non-profits. One goal: helping people adapt to life in Germany.
partnerships.hero.title: Partnerships
partnerships.hero.intro: Simple Deutsch is open to collaboration with everyone: individuals, teams, companies, and non-profits. We are united by one goal: helping people adapt more easily to life in Germany through clear language and practical skills.
partnerships.partners.heading: Our partners
partnerships.sections.preLogos.p1: We make learning simple and useful, without overload, with a focus on what really matters in real life.
partnerships.sections.preLogos.p2:
Together, we can:

launch educational programs
create useful content
support local initiatives
partnerships.sections.preLogos.p3: If you have an idea or a challenge, we would be happy to collaborate. It does not matter whether you are an individual or an organization.
partnerships.sections.postLogos.p1:
We usually start with a simple goal, for example:

improve access to language
support integration
build digital skills
partnerships.sections.postLogos.p2: Then we quickly put together a clear plan: what we do, who is responsible for what, and what result we want to achieve.
partnerships.cta.title: Let's collaborate
partnerships.cta.lead: We believe in an easy start, honest collaboration, and real results.
